programmes.show_description: Alice covers Tara's childish crayon, Glen rids Alex of his hero-worshipping howler, Sketch helps tame Aaron's little monster and Jay suffers a case of beard envy.
Emotions run high as Bridget Christie shoves a cuddly toy inside a traffic cone. Sophie Duker does a strange dinosaur impression. Plus: robots, and an impressive hose!
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson take on Lee Mack and Katherine Ryan. Brett Domino joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Nader Masadeh, the president and CEO of Buffalo Wings & Rings, goes undercover in his own company to ensure that his standards are being kept across the brand.
Shawnon Bellah, chief operating officer of Nestlé Toll House Café by Chip, works undercover among her staff, learning about cookie decoration and the drive-thru experience.
...Selling: Overworked nurse Olivia and fireman Dan are desperate for a fresh start. Can the brothers prescribe the remedy needed to bring their current home back to life?
In Essex, there are deep-fried jam sandwiches and some attention-grabbing burlesque, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party.
Three couples put their hosting skills to the test on Jersey, in a week featuring wedding talk, a chinchilla dinner guest and cocktails from a fake cow...
The Midsummer Night's Wedding One: James longs for a traditional big day, so how will he react when groom Jack books a cast of actors to perform a Shakespeare classic?

programmes.show_name: Don't Tell the Bride: Let It Snow
programmes.show_description: Steve from Rotherham plans a wedding with an icy twist for his bride Emma, who wants an impressive, upmarket affair. But will his big ideas leave him snowed under?
Cindy Galardi Culpepper, the CEO and chairperson of Wienerschnitzel, the world's largest hot dog chain, goes incognito among her staff to look at the company's communication.
Lowell Hawthorne, the president and CEO of Caribbean food producer Golden Krust, goes undercover among the workforce in his own company, to determine the state of the business.
Gordon Returns: Gordon Ramsay revisits some of the restaurants he set out to save, including The Mixing Bowl, The Olde Stone Mill, Peter's and Campania. Have things improved?
Hot Potato Café: The Hot Potato Café in Philadelphia has never made a profit. When the chips are down, can Gordon help save the restaurant?
...and Selling: Brand new series: Sheryl and Frank's renovations may be the least of the brothers' problems. Instead, earning Frank's trust proves to be as tough as nails.
Cat's Out of the Bag: Jared's in hot water, Ben finds himself in a difficult position, a stew gets troubling news, and a shock decision rocks the boat.
Alex Horne is pelted with milk and gin, Bridget Christie has many questions about shoes, Judi Love is bewildered by bunting, and Ardal O'Hanlon looks a bit bereft.
Jimmy Carr hosts, as Sarah Millican and Nish Kumar take on Jon Richardson and Maisie Adam. David O'Doherty joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts, as Roisin Conaty and Asim Chaudhry take on Jon Richardson and Josh Widdicombe. Bill Bailey joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
The Test: Jeremy meets a new love, Russian émigré Elena. Meanwhile, Mark makes a final play for Dobby, the object of his desires. But Sophie has some news...
Jeremy in Love: Jeremy realises that he's in love with Elena and decides to be less selfish to win her affection. But is it a good idea to tell her? Mark isn't so sure...
Jay gives Ieuan a tribute to his late sister, Sketch evicts the eye on Mike's thigh, Glen tackles Ben's boastful ink, and Alice decorates Abbie's drunken doodle.
Alex Horne is pelted with milk and gin, Bridget Christie has many questions about shoes, Judi Love is bewildered by bunting, and Ardal O'Hanlon looks a bit bereft.
Jimmy Carr hosts, as Sarah Millican and Nish Kumar take on Jon Richardson and Maisie Adam. David O'Doherty joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Sam Dushey, the CEO of Shoppers World, goes incognito among his own workforce, seeking reassurance that his stores are performing to his high standards as the business grows.
Tim Betts, the CEO of Muscle Maker Grill, goes undercover in the restaurant chain that he recently bought. Is every branch following the same high standards?
...Selling: Charlie, Renee and their daughters love cooking. So, a dream kitchen is at the top of their wish list. Can Drew and Jonathan add some flavour to their current place?
In Plymouth, Christine and Kevin serve a menu that even Christine isn't convinced about. Harvey and Natasha throw a Spanish fiesta. And will nerves get the better of Paul and Jo?
In south London, there are evil glares, a rooftop rave and a cooking meltdown on the hottest day of the year, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party.
The One with the Wannabe DJ Groom: Bride Ellis has dreamed her whole life of a quiet church wedding. Luke decides to plan a rave-inspired do...
The One on Top of a Mountain: Richard's gambling everything on a Welsh mountain-top wedding for his bride Jody, but even his best man is unsure about his plans.
Will it be child's play for Sharon John, CEO of Build-A-Bear Workshop, as she works undercover alongside the employees who custom-make stuffed toy animals in her retail outlets?
Karen Freeman-Wilson, the mayor of Gary in Indiana, goes incognito among the city's workforce and meets a sanitation plant operator, a firefighter and a police officer.
Flamangos: Gordon visits Flamangos in rural New Jersey, where mother and daughter Adele and Cheryl are stuck in their tropical ways. But their restaurant is far from paradise.
Bazzini: Award-winning chef Paul runs Italian joint Bazzini in New Jersey, but didn't realise how difficult running a restaurant would be. Can Gordon turn it around?
...and Selling: Brand new series: After buying their home at a top market price, Michelle and Danielle don't want to lose money. Will a huge renovation secure a high-selling sum?
Murder on the High Seas: Ben's mistake leaves everyone stranded at night in the middle of a storm. A new deckhand draws attention. And Fraser shares a kiss.
The comics are baffled by high fives and a fish tank. Bridget Christie has a present for the Taskmaster, and Sophie Duker covers some keys in saliva.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Lee Mack and Joe Wilkinson take on Jon Richardson and Jessica Knappett. Comedian Nigel Ng joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts the comedic mash-up. Jack Dee and Tom Allen take on Jon Richardson and Judi Love. Dr John Cooper Clarke joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
The Affair: Mark discovers Jeremy's girlfriend Elena has a guilty secret, but should Mark tell Jez about this when he seems so happy? Elena doesn't think it's such a good idea!
The Party: As a last desperate hurrah before the baby arrives, Mark and Jeremy decide to throw a party.
